label: Scope of Work
description: Fixed-scope or T&M statement of work for a client engagement.

fields.label: Project summary
fields.help: One to three paragraphs describing the project at a high level.

fields.label: Fee structure
fields.placeholder: Fixed fee $X — or — T&M at $Y/hr, not-to-exceed $Z

generation_notes:
Voice: Consulting-professional. Clear, direct, unambiguous about scope and deliverables. Defensive about assumptions.

Structure: Each section is mandatory and should be completed even if user input is thin — where thin, use precise placeholder language like "To be confirmed in writing within 5 business days of execution" rather than padding.

Required elements:
- Executive Summary (3-5 sentences, no more)
- Scope (bulleted list of in-scope work items with 1-2 sentence each description)
- Deliverables (numbered list, each with a concrete output description and acceptance criteria)
- Timeline (key milestones with target dates or "TBD" if not provided)
- Assumptions & Exclusions (explicit list — what's NOT in scope)
- Fees & Terms (restate user input precisely; add standard "payment net 30 from invoice date" if not specified)
- Acceptance (single sentence explaining how deliverables are accepted)

Tone examples:
  CORRECT: "Consultant will deliver a technical architecture document, a data model ERD, and a phased implementation roadmap. Acceptance requires written sign-off from Client's designated project owner."
  WRONG: "We'll work together to create an amazing architecture that will revolutionize your business!"

Never invent deliverables the user didn't specify. If input is vague, reflect that with "to be defined jointly during kickoff" rather than inventing specifics.

Length target: 600-1200 words.
